headline: AI Chip Demand Sustains; Trump Policy & Macro Data Drive Market Volatility
summary: US indices saw mixed movements as robust AI chip demand continued to underpin tech, while macro data and evolving Trump policy narratives introduced volatility. Gold prices held gains following weak US jobs data, tempering rate hike expectations.

bullets.lead: Intel targets TSMC's CoWoS lead with new technology.

segments.text: Intel is developing EMIB-T technology to challenge TSMC's dominance in CoWoS advanced packaging, indicating intensified competition in high-performance chip integration.

sources.title: Intel takes aim at TSMC's CoWoS lead with EMIB-T

bullets.lead: Samsung Foundry reports strong capacity utilization.

segments.text: Samsung Foundry's 4nm capacity is reportedly sold out through next year, with 8nm processes at full utilization, leading to selective order taking for large clients.

bullets.lead: GPU supply remains tight despite spot market signals.

segments.text: Despite some reports of lower GPU spot prices, large-cloud and enterprise demand for H100 and B300 GPUs remains strong and supply-constrained.

bullets.lead: Micron CEO projects long-term memory demand.

segments.text: Micron CEO Sanjay Mehrotra highlighted strong, secular long-term demand trends for memory driven by AI, projecting supply tightness beyond 2027.

bullets.lead: Trump's crypto earnings draw scrutiny.

segments.text: Former President Trump's reported $1 billion earnings from crypto assets are facing criticism, with Democrats plotting subpoenas over his financial activities.

sources.title: Americans disgusted at Trump earning $1bn from crypto as president: ‘Obviously a grift’
Democrats plot subpoena storm over Trump's $2 billion gold rush

bullets.lead: Trump expects SpaceX stock donations.

segments.text: Former President Trump has indicated he expects Elon Musk to donate SpaceX stock to "Trump Accounts," despite past disagreements.

sources.title: Trump says he expects Musk to donate SpaceX stock to Trump Accounts, despite their 'little dispute'

bullets.lead: OpenAI engages Trump administration.

segments.text: OpenAI is reportedly courting the Trump administration as its latest investor, signaling potential strategic alignment with US policy.

sources.title: OpenAI courts Trump administration as its latest investor

bullets.lead: Jones Act lifted by Trump.

segments.text: The lifting of the century-old Jones Act by the Trump administration impacted fuel markets, demonstrating policy's direct effect on commodity flows.

sources.title: What Happened in Fuel Markets When Trump Lifted the Century-Old Jones Act

bullets.lead: Weak US jobs data lowers rate hike odds.

segments.text: Gold prices held gains as weaker-than-expected US jobs data reduced the probability of further rate hikes.

sources.title: Gold Holds Gains As Weak US Jobs Data Lowers Rate Hike Odds

bullets.lead: Copper climbs on fading rate hike prospects.

segments.text: Industrial metals, including copper, saw gains as prospects for additional rate hikes began to fade.

segments.text: JPMorgan projects gold prices could reach $4,500 in the fourth quarter, though noting potential downside risks.

segments.text: The June jobs report indicated the US labor market is making slower but steady gains, aligning with a moderating economic outlook.

bullets.lead: Microsoft launches $2.5B AI unit.

segments.text: Microsoft has established a new $2.5 billion unit aimed at guiding enterprise AI adoption through embedded engineers.

sources.title: Microsoft launches US$2.5 B unit to guide enterprise AI adoption using embedded engineers

segments.text: Google lost its appeal against a record €4.1 billion Android antitrust fine, highlighting ongoing regulatory pressures on major tech platforms.

x_themes.topic: AI market downturn analysis and bull case
x_themes.summary: The current AI market downturn is attributed to Broadcom's guidance, hawkish Fed sentiment, and 'AI bubble' commentary, but underlying physical demand for wafers, HBM, CoWoS, and optics remains strong and supply-constrained. Hyperscaler capex is projected to rise, and the shift to inference-led demand suggests reliable recurring revenue, indicating the sell-off is a reset rather than a fundamental demand collapse.

x_themes.topic: Samsung Foundry's resurgence and capacity allocation
x_themes.summary: Samsung Foundry is experiencing a strong comeback, with 4nm capacity sold out through next year and 8nm processes at full utilization, leading to selective order taking for large customers and potential price hikes of 15-20%.

x_themes.topic: Coherent (COHR) secures 6-inch InP wafer supply from AXT (AXTI)
x_themes.summary: Coherent signed a 3-year Master Development and Supply Agreement with AXT-Tongmei for 6-inch indium phosphide (InP) wafer substrates, involving a $22.3M prepayment to secure capacity expansion at AXT's Beijing facility. This de-risks Coherent's AI optical ramp by locking in a critical upstream supply.

x_themes.topic: Persistent GPU supply tightness despite misleading spot market signals
x_themes.summary: Despite some reports of lower GPU spot prices, large-cloud and enterprise demand for H100 and B300 GPUs remains strong and supply-constrained, with AWS pricing firm and neocloud providers reporting demand significantly exceeding supply.

x_themes.topic: Custom AI chip proliferation and manufacturing bottlenecks
x_themes.summary: The proliferation of custom AI chips from hyperscalers like Meta and Amazon is highlighting persistent manufacturing bottlenecks in areas such as advanced packaging, substrates, and PCB materials, shifting the focus from chip design to the capacity required to build and package these custom silicon solutions.

x_themes.topic: MediaTek pursuing Meta AI accelerator deal and expanding custom ASIC business
x_themes.summary: MediaTek is actively pursuing an AI accelerator chip deal with Meta and is expanding its custom ASIC business, with Google as its primary client for chips like Zebrafish and Humufish, aiming for $10 billion in revenue.

x_themes.topic: Micron (MU) CEO on strong, long-term memory demand and supply tightness
x_themes.summary: Micron CEO Sanjay Mehrotra highlighted strong, secular long-term demand trends for memory driven by AI, projecting supply tightness beyond 2027, and discussed Micron's significant investments and strategic customer agreements to meet this demand.

x_themes.topic: Agility Robotics' leading position in humanoid commercialization
x_themes.summary: Agility Robotics, accessible via CCXI, is highlighted as the leading humanoid player in the US by commercialization, with deployments in Amazon and Mercado Libre, and backed by major investors.

x_themes.topic: Meta's (META) NeoCloud strategy and tiered AI compute infrastructure
x_themes.summary: Meta's NeoCloud initiative and consideration of external compute commercialization do not signal peak AI demand, but rather a shift to a tiered AI infrastructure where older GPUs are repurposed for inference while next-generation systems like GB300 remain scarce for frontier training.

x_themes.topic: Underestimation of Chinese AI capex, ByteDance's $100B spending
x_themes.summary: Chinese AI capex is likely underestimated by focusing solely on big tech, with ByteDance alone projected to spend over $100B next year, and contributions from SOEs and telecom operators needing to be included.

x_themes.topic: Tesla (TSLA) caps employee AI spending at $200/week
x_themes.summary: Tesla is reportedly implementing a $200 per week cap on AI spending for its employees, with exceptions for xAI beta products, after some software engineers were using thousands of dollars worth of AI tokens weekly.

x_themes.topic: Nvidia's Nemotron development and post-training stack
x_themes.summary: Nvidia is actively developing its Nemotron post-training stack, having started serious efforts in Q4 last year and recently releasing Nemotron v4 Ultra, with expectations to match competitor models by Q4.

x_themes.topic: Ams Osram (AMS) pivots to AI photonics via MicroLED after non-optical segment sale
x_themes.summary: Ams Osram sold its non-optical segment to Infineon for €570M to de-risk its balance sheet and fund a pivot into AI photonics, specifically MicroLED technology, which could be a beneficiary if adopted in racks.

x_themes.topic: Marvell (MRVL) revenue growth driven by Google CXL business
x_themes.summary: Marvell is projected to see significant revenue growth, reaching $13.0B by 2028, primarily driven by its Google CXL-related business.
